Workgroup 4 · Meeting 3 of 3

Final Prep — Round 2 Results & Conference Logistics

Human-AI Interaction & the Perception of Self

Wednesday, May 6, 2026 · 3:00–3:45 PM ET · Virtual · 45 min · Virtual

●

Meeting materials not yet released

The co-leads will release this meeting's agenda, discussion materials, and (when available) live Round results closer to the meeting date.

If you're a WG4 member and believe you should have access, please contact Tehreem Rehman at tehreem.rehman@mountsinai.org.

← Back to Delphi Platform

SAEM 2026 AI Consensus Conference · Workgroup 4

Workgroup 4 · Meeting 3 of 3

Final Prep — Round 2 Results & Conference Logistics

Human-AI Interaction & the Perception of Self

Wednesday, May 6, 2026 · 3:00–3:45 PM ET · Virtual · 45 min · Virtual

Agenda

TimeTopic
3:00–3:03Welcome & objectives for the final virtual meeting
3:03–3:13Round 2 results: what moved, what stuck, what's locked in (live data)
3:13–3:23Final slate: walkthrough of confirmed research priorities
3:23–3:30Pending items: how we'll handle them in the in-person breakout
3:30–3:38May 21 logistics: breakout assignments, cross-group exchange, plenary vote
3:38–3:42Day-of roles: speakers, scribe, vote captains
3:42–3:45Q&A and final wrap-up

Final Stretch

Round 2 is closed. The live tallies below show our final pre-conference state. Anything still PENDING after Round 2 will go to the in-person breakout for discussion and a full-audience vote in Atlanta.

May 21 — What to Expect in Atlanta

Venue: Atlanta Marriott Marquis (during the SAEM Annual Meeting)

Format:

  • Morning breakouts — each of the 5 working groups convenes to walk its final slate, address any PENDING items, and rehearse its presentation.
  • Cross-group discussion — representatives from each WG share priorities and surface cross-cutting themes (especially overlap between WG4 and WG3 ‘education’ and WG5 ‘ethics’).
  • Full-audience consensus vote — all ~150 attendees vote on each research priority. ≥80% threshold to include in the final 10-year agenda.

What We Need from You On the Day

  • Arrive 15 minutes early; coffee and badges at the WG4 table
  • Bring a laptop or tablet for the live vote
  • Review the final slate (above) the night before
  • Identify 1–2 questions you feel strongest about and be ready to speak briefly to them

After the Conference

The consensus output becomes the foundation for a peer-reviewed manuscript and a 10-year AI-in-EM research agenda. WG4 members are listed as co-authors and may contribute to drafting the human-factors section.
